operatingPrinciples: Keep Moodle as the student-facing entry point during the pilot.
Use the assistant for support, clarification, study guidance, and LMS navigation help.
Do not use the assistant for grading, official academic decisions, or full assignment writing.
Escalate academic, LMS, technical, privacy, and accessibility concerns to named project-team owners.
Stop or pause the pilot if student experience, safety, privacy, or support readiness is in doubt.

roles.name: Project Sponsor
roles.responsibility: Approve pilot continuation, escalation decisions, and stakeholder communication.

roles.name: Project Lead and Technical Lead
roles.responsibility: Coordinate pilot operations, technical readiness, evidence review, and go/no-go recommendations.

roles.name: Developer
roles.responsibility: Support application behavior, endpoints, bug fixes, and documented limitations.

roles.name: LMS Administrator
roles.responsibility: Manage Moodle course placement, embed behavior, fallback links, and LMS navigation issues.

roles.name: System Administrator and Infrastructure Support
roles.responsibility: Support hosting, environment variables, provider connectivity, logs, and infrastructure availability.

procedures.title: Pre-session readiness check
steps: Run the pilot smoke test against the active pilot URL.
Review deployment readiness warnings and confirm no blocking failures.
Confirm the provider test responds for the selected OpenAI or Ollama provider.
Review the pilot evidence dashboard, action register, and accessibility usability review.
Confirm Moodle embed placement and fallback link with the LMS administrator.
outputs: Known warnings accepted for controlled pilot

procedures.title: Live pilot monitoring
steps: Observe whether students remain inside Moodle while using the assistant.
Confirm the responsible-use notice is visible and understandable.
Watch for repeated missing-answer, LMS navigation, or assignment-integrity questions.
Capture student feedback using the built-in feedback controls.
Record any issue that requires lecturer, LMS, technical, accessibility, or knowledge base follow-up.

procedures.title: Student support flow
steps: For LMS access or navigation issues, route the student to the LMS administrator.
For assignment interpretation or official course guidance, route the student to the lecturer.
For AI misuse or academic integrity concerns, reinforce responsible-use expectations and escalate to the lecturer.
For missing or unclear assistant answers, capture the question as a knowledge base review item.
For technical/provider errors, pause use if needed and notify the technical lead.

procedures.title: Pause or stop-pilot trigger
steps: Pause the pilot if Moodle embedding fails for the pilot group.
Pause the pilot if the assistant produces repeated unsafe, misleading, or assignment-writing responses.
Pause the pilot if privacy, accessibility, or support concerns cannot be handled during the session.
Switch students to the fallback support path if the AI provider or hosted app is unavailable.
Document the trigger, owner, immediate response, and decision before resuming.

procedures.title: Post-session review
steps: Review pilot feedback, launch audit, and support issues.
Update the admin action register with owners, priority, source, and due date.
Convert repeated missing-answer issues into knowledge base change requests.
Update pilot meeting pack and pilot report exports for project-team review.
Decide whether to continue iframe testing, move to Moodle block review, or hold for fixes.

stopPilotTriggers: Assistant is unavailable for the pilot group and fallback support is not ready.
Students cannot access the assistant inside Moodle and are required to leave the approved course interface.
Repeated responses appear to write full assignments or imply grading authority.
Privacy, accessibility, or cybersecurity concerns are raised and cannot be resolved during the session.
Project sponsor, project lead, lecturer, LMS administrator, or IT support path requests a pause.
postSessionQuestions: Did students remain inside Moodle while using the assistant?
Did students understand what the assistant can and cannot do?
Were any questions outside the knowledge base repeated?
Were support issues routed to the correct owner?
Should the next test remain iframe-based, move to Moodle block review, or pause for fixes?
governanceNotes: This runbook is a Phase 2 pilot operations artifact, not a production incident management system.
Do not record student names, IDs, grades, disability details, or protected student record information in this runbook.
Use formal institutional policies for production incident response, accessibility accommodations, privacy review, and academic misconduct handling.
